title: Hiyori and Yasuie Reveal Wano’s Pain
description: On pages 2–5, this part focuses on Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Yasuie, Ebisu Town Citizens, Toko, Unknown person #3 as Hiyori’s identity and Yasuie’s sacrifice pull the Wano rebellion into a deeper emotional crisis.
scene: Zoro protects Toko and meets Hiyori, while Yasuie prepares a sacrifice meant to rescue the rebellion’s plan.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Don't forget that you doubted my friends today.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Watching my back on the line between life and death!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There's no way I can have someone I don't trust

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Aaarrghh! Traffy got all pissed and stormed out.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I hope he comes back.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Why'd Shinobu have to say all that stuff?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They really have been waiting for this day for 20 years.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I mean, I understand where she's coming from, though.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even if bepo and the others leaked our plans,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I wouldn't blame 'em.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Yeah, well... you'd talk too.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: And so would you!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hmm... torture? I'd last maybe three seconds.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That's it?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Get out of my sight!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: I won't help lowlifies like you lot!

dialogue.speaker: Yasuie
dialogue.text: Huh?! An angry voice in ebisu town?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: That's a newcomer.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He was just kicked out of the capital yesterday. He's originally from there, apparently.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: THERE YOU ARE! THE CRAZY SPIRIT LADY.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Quit it.

dialogue.speaker: Ebisu Town Citizens
dialogue.text: What're you laugh- ing at?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Kyaha Haha!

dialogue.speaker: Toko
dialogue.text: Wahaha haha!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Kicked out of the Capital?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Only people fiercely loyal to the Shogun and that can basically pull money out of their rear end are allowed to live in the flower capital.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But those guys were up to some pretty bad business!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: night after night, the underlying, bungo, would set fire to buildings around town.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He was paying off officials, so he never was caught or punished.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: An Arsonist?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Why was he paying off officials?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So his friends could make money off the fires.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Bongo ran a lumber store that sold wood beams used in house repair.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: In

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and bingo the monk and mortician would take advantage of grieving families

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: that lost loved ones in the fires and charge them exorbitant prices.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Bingo, Bongo, and Bungo were in Cahoots.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But because they had bribed officials, they'd never be caught.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Anyhowever you have to

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The three of them were also- lately besotted with the legendary beauty that died the other day, Komurasaki.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So much so that they bankrupted themselves patronizing her.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I guess someone really is watchin' up there!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Or so the rumor goes.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's a pretty specific rumor! It's all true, isn't it?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I bet that courtesan knew all about what they were doing! Amazing! Who is she?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I knew I'd seen them before! Those guys were going wild during the courtesan's procession!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: That's why!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The three of them are staying in the house that Yasuie lives in.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even though he's been helping them, they're still on their high horse.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Tonyasu sure does love helping people.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Even if they're bad to the bone!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: It's true. Thanks to this little card,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We learned about the day of the final battle, whether or not that was intended.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: I Gym- Pathize.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: you're plan's fallen apart. The enemy's aware of it now.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: MM... you're right.

dialogue.speaker: Kinemon
dialogue.text: But your allies might believe that too.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But I didn't even know about this reverse crescent moon on the ankle thing. That's the kind of allies you have.

summary: This section advances the Wano - Hiyori and Yasuie storyline with 58 dialogue beats. Hiyori and Yasuie bring the rebellion’s emotional history into the foreground.
